name: openai
description: Query OpenAI models, generate text responses, embeddings, and images via the OpenAI API

tools.name: List Models
tools.description: List the models available to the API key

historicalSummary.omitted: model capability details and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Model
tools.description: Get details about a specific model

model.description: Model ID, e.g. gpt-4o or o4-mini

tools.name: Create Response
tools.description: Generate a model response using the OpenAI Responses API

model.description: Model ID to use, e.g. gpt-4o

input.description: Text input or prompt for the model

tools.name: Create Chat Completion
tools.description: Generate a chat completion from a list of messages

model.description: Model ID to use, e.g. gpt-4o

messages.description: Chat messages, each with role (system|user|assistant) and content

tools.name: Create Embedding
tools.description: Create an embedding vector for input text

dimensions.description: Number of dimensions for the output embedding (supported models only)

tools.name: Generate Image
tools.description: Generate an image from a text prompt

model.description: Image model ID, e.g. gpt-image-1 or dall-e-3

prompt.description: Text description of the desired image

prompts.title: List available models
prompts.prompt: List the OpenAI models available to my API key and summarize what each is best for.

prompts.title: Generate a response
prompts.prompt: Use OpenAI to generate a response to a prompt I provide.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: OpenAI API Setup

steps.title: Create an OpenAI account
steps.description: Go to https://platform.openai.com and sign up or sign in. New accounts may need to add billing details under Settings → Billing before API calls succeed.

steps.title: Create an API key
steps.description: Open https://platform.openai.com/api-keys, click 'Create new secret key', give it a descriptive name (e.g. 'Veryfront Integration'), and select the project it belongs to.

steps.title: Copy and store the key
steps.description: Copy the key immediately — it is only shown once. Add it to your .env file as OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the List Models tool to confirm the key works. If you get a 401, check the key; if a 429, check your billing/quota.

notes: Use project-scoped API keys to limit blast radius; you can revoke keys at any time from the dashboard
API usage is billed per token — set usage limits under Settings → Limits
